conflicts.description: Existing sheet estimated roughly 10 months ending in early spring 2028; manuscript anchors dated events in June/July 2027 and still-winter conditions by chapters 17-18.
conflicts.canonical_value: About 7 to 8 months, from late spring/early summer 2027 through winter immediate aftermath.
conflicts.resolution_note: Date-stamped entries plus repeated winter markers are embedded in major turning points, so the shorter span is more causally constrained.

conflicts.description: Mercury Night aftermath reports diverge: official bulletin totals conflict with Ina's independently verified field ledger totals.
conflicts.canonical_value: Carry both source-labeled totals: official bulletin (7 dead, 32 injured, 91 relocated) and independent field tally (26 dead, 59 injured, 143 screening detentions).
conflicts.resolution_note: Both values drive later behavior (public narrative vs accountability archive); collapsing to one number would break causality.

conflicts.description: Prior sheet treated council stipend and media residuals as relatively stable until mid-cycle; manuscript shows explicit suspension leverage, freeze events, and full income collapse by chapter 16.
conflicts.canonical_value: Treat council stipend and MiraeWave residual income as politically revocable and effectively lost by chapter 16.
conflicts.resolution_note: The later explicit income-loss statements and account freezes are operationally determinative and override earlier implied stability.
